title: Hundreds of Math Professors Ask UC to Bring Back SAT/ACT Requirements
snippet: Ahmet Palazoglu, chair of the systemwide Academic Senate, also told the Times in a statement that he has heard 'concerns raised by UC faculty about student preparedness for undergraduate study,' and he has called on the system admissions board to address 'timely topics tied to students' college readiness and UC's admission process.'
match_reason: websearch (press_quote) — Palazoglu is quoted by name; article covers AI tools as a factor undermining standardized assessment and his role responding to this as Academic Senate chair.

title: #FutureofEducation #AI — Beware of AI NOW or AI NEVER
snippet: Beware of AI NOW or AI NEVER. My takeaways from this smart analysis: 1. Students must be involved in the rollout of an AI strategy. 2. Students and educators should know that refusal to use AI without an engagement strategy is a losing strategy. 3. We are in the Bermuda Triangle moment of AI adoption.
match_reason: social (social_post) — Post authored by Ann Kirschner (linkedin.com/in/annkirschner), commenting on AI adoption in higher education, dated '2d ago' relative to 2026-06-05.

title: SIGMOD 2026 Panel P3: Sustaining Data Management Research in the GenAI Era
snippet: Panel P3 brings together representatives from funding agencies, industry, and senior researchers from across the globe to discuss how the data management community can articulate its value to funders, navigate constrained government budgets, engage productively with industry... Panelists: Rajesh Gupta (Dean, SCIDS, UCSD)

title: UC San Diego Halıcıoğlu School MDS Online Program — Data Science and AI Expertise
snippet: Rajesh Gupta shared: Data science opportunities just expanded. The UC San Diego Halıcıoğlu School of Data Science and Computing is now welcoming applications from New York and Delaware residents for the Master of Data Science online program. Gain advanced data science and AI expertise through a flexible, fully online graduate program built for today's professionals.

title: UC Berkeley Law School Adopts New, Strict Ban On AI Use By Students
snippet: UC Berkeley Law has adopted a new policy banning students' use of AI for most academic work submitted for credit, effective Summer 2026. The rule prohibits AI for conceptualizing, outlining, drafting, revising, translating, or editing submitted work, and bars any AI use in exams.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — Direct UC campus AI academic-integrity and assessment policy change at a UC R1 law school, squarely within the committee's governance and academic-integrity mandate.

title: Berkeley Law's AI crackdown highlights chatbot concerns
snippet: Starting this summer, UC Berkeley School of Law students are barred from using AI to brainstorm paper topics, generate exam outlines, summarize legal rules, or correct grammar. The Reuters piece covers the broader legal-education debate on AI's effect on foundational lawyering skills.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — Mainstream-press coverage of UC Berkeley's new AI academic-integrity policy and the wider higher-education debate it has sparked, directly relevant to the committee's charge on AI governance and assessment.

title: IT Workers Join UPTE-CWA, Form Largest Tech Union in the Country
snippet: 2,100 UC information technology professionals voted overwhelmingly to join UPTE, forming the largest tech-worker union in the US with 8,400 total members, explicitly citing AI deployment and job-security concerns as organizing drivers.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — Updates the UC system AI governance story with confirmed unionization outcome, relevant to how UC will negotiate AI policy with represented technical staff.

title: How a Professor Used AI to Teach a Complex Concept
snippet: Chronicle of Higher Education Teaching newsletter covers how instructors are now creating interactive AI chatbots to explain difficult concepts to students, with faculty saying the tools are advanced enough for substantive pedagogical deployment.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — Trade-press coverage of generative AI use in university teaching and learning, directly relevant to the committee's instructional mandate.
